mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal expanding household income and consumption, which increases corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131708K as of t-1mo indicates employment level
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month increases in PAYEMS are not positive in more than 50% of out-of-sample months, or the mean conditional return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, elevated labor slack generates expectations of prolonged monetary accommodation and fiscal support, compressing the equity risk premium and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.6% as of t-1mo exceeds 8.0% threshold
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative or insignificantly different from zero in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 8.0%, or the mean conditional return is below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and declines in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) validate a self-reinforcing consumer demand and labor income loop that amplifies equity demand beyond single-series effects, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 399349M and UNRATE at 8.6% as of recent prints
failure_condition: The joint signal does not produce higher mean 20-day returns than the sum of independent marginal effects, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a predictive regression.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month declines in industrial production (INDPRO) signal emerging capacity constraints and margin compression, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in late-cycle periods are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) lead residential construction activity, employment in building trades, and durable goods consumption, creating a demand cascade that predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of rolling 3-year windows, or the Pearson correlation between HOUST changes and forward returns is negative and significant.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and a falling consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) reflects a sharp increase in real interest rates, which tightens financial conditions disproportionately and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% and CPIAUCSL at 226.763 as of recent prints
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising FEDFUNDS and falling CPIAUCSL fails to generate negative mean 20-day returns significantly lower (t-test p < 0.05) than the return under rising FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) rises by more than 0.5% over a three-month span, accelerating inflation erodes real corporate earnings and triggers expectations of monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances of CPIAUCSL rising >0.5% in 3 months are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mean conditional return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Positive growth in real GDP (GDPC1) confirms aggregate demand strength and resource utilization, leading to upward revisions in earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly increases in GDPC1 are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample observations, or the Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward returns is below 0.02.
